message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

# SP_0002.1 — SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser

  >   resolveContextPath (nested path + array index support)
  >   interpolateString ({{var}} regex replacement)
  >   interpolateDeep (recursive object/array walk)

  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIInterpolation.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
- Existing interpolation example in RFC:

  → "Voucher para Abastecimento"
- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used

- [DONE] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1–3.2).
  > Registry at src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts — createRegistry, registerComponent,
  >   getRenderer, registerToDefault (immutable API + mutable default singleton).
  > ScreenRenderer at src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx — renders header,
  >   body[], footer from SDUIScreen JSON. Applies interpolateDeep to all props.
  > FallbackComponent at src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx — visible
  >   yellow box for unknown component types (uses RN primitives for ThemeProvider safety).
  > 14 unit tests passing (registry.test.ts + ScreenRenderer.test.tsx).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIScreenRenderer.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Registry maps string type → React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1

Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
screen definition from JSON.

  | icon_text_row    | Box + Icon + Text         | Regras de uso bullet items |
  | section_heading  | Text (subtitleXXL)        | Regras section titles      |

# SP_0003.2 — Storybook JSON Editor Addon (IndexedDB Persistence)
- [DONE] Create a custom Storybook addon panel that allows editing the SDUI JSON
      payload in real-time, with automatic persistence to IndexedDB.
  > 6 files in .storybook/addons/json-editor/:
  >   register.tsx (addon registration), JsonEditorPanel.tsx (editor UI),
  >   useSDUIJsonEditor.ts (preview-side hook), useIndexedDB.ts (persistence),
  >   constants.ts (shared IDs/events), index.ts (barrel exports).
  > Features: textarea with monospace font, Format/Reset buttons, debounced
  >   auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID, JSON validation with inline errors,
  >   "edited" badge when payload differs from default, live preview via channel events.
  > Integrated in main.ts addons array + used by SDUIDemoVoucher stories.
  > Review fixes applied: IDB error handling (.catch), db.close() on cleanup,
  >   debounce timer cleanup on unmount.
  > Screenshot validated: json-editor-validated.png.

Context:
- Every SDUI story should have a companion JSON editor panel
- The editor should:
  a. Show the current SDUIScreen JSON in a textarea or code editor
  b. Parse and validate JSON on change (show errors inline)
  c. Apply changes live to the ScreenRenderer preview
  d. Auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID so edits persist across refreshes
  e. Have a "Reset to Default" button to restore the original mock payload
- Implementation approach:
  a. Create .storybook/addons/json-editor/ with:
     - register.tsx (Storybook addon registration)
     - JsonEditorPanel.tsx (the editor UI component)
     - useIndexedDB.ts (IndexedDB read/write hook)
  b. Register as a panel addon in .storybook/main.ts
  c. Use Storybook's addons API: addons.register(), addons.addPanel()
  d. Communicate with stories via Storybook channel events
- Reference: https://storybook.js.org/docs/addons/writing-addons
- The editor should work with ALL SDUI stories (current and future)
- Consider using a simple <textarea> with monospace font for MVP — no need for
  a full Monaco editor initially
- IndexedDB key format: `sdui-json-${storyId}`

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
